0.q: The SOS1 gene was originally identified in Arabidopsis as one main component of the SOS (salt overly sensitive) signal transduction pathway (also including SOS2 and SOS3) and involved in maintaining cellular Na+ homeostasis in plants under salt stress.

ABI4 down-regulated the Na+ transporter HKT1;1 expression, indicating that a plant's salt tolerance is related to its ability to reduce sodium accumulation in the aerial parts.
HKT1;1 was found to be negatively regulated by the ABA- and salt-regulated TF ABA INSENSITIVE 4.

128.q: The dysfunction of MNS1 and MNS2 leads to a decreased stability and abundance of RADIALLY SWOLLEN 2/KORRIGAN1 (RSW2/KOR), a glycoprotein crucial for cellulose biosynthesis.

abundance of a membrane anchored endo-b-1,4-endoglucanase (RSW2/KOR)...were modulated by a-1,2-mannose trimming under salt stress. Overexpression of RSW2 could partially rescue the salt-sensitive phenotype of mns1 mns2
dysfunction of Golgi a1,2-mannosidase (MNS1 and MNS2)...leads to a decreased stability and abundance of RSW2/KOR1, a glycoprotein crucial for cellulose biosynthesis.

129.q: EIN2 positively regulates the functions of EIN3/EIL1 transcription factors; salt treatment promoted EIN3 and EIL1 proteins accumulation, although not as dramatic, even in ein2-5 mutant background.

In the sos2 mutant, we observed compromised proteolytic processing of EIN2 and reduced nuclear localization of EIN2-C, which correlate with decreased accumulation of EIN3.

130.q: CO and FT transcript levels were remarkably reduced by salt stress in WT, and their levels were low and not affected further by salt in gi-1, providing an explanation for the abrogation of NaCl-induced delay of flowering in the gi-1 mutant.
The ability of GI to negatively regulate salinity tolerance requires a functional CO. The transgenic overexpression of CO largely rescued the salinity-tolerant phenotype of gi-1.
Salt stress delays flowering in Arabidopsis wild type as a result of reduced transcript levels for CO and FT. GI protein amount is drastically decreased upon salt treatment.

131.q: SOS2 kinase activity was reduced in the scabp8 mutant compared to the wild-type under salt stress... SCaBP8 is required to activate salt-induced, calcium-dependent SOS2 kinase activity
the calcium binding proteins SOS3 and SOS3-LIKE CALCIUM BINDING PROTEIN8 (SCaBP8) nonredundantly activate the protein kinase SOS2.
enhance SOS2 activity in a calcium-dependent manner.

132.q: MKK5DD seedlings … were transplanted to plates containing 150 mM NaCl … to induce the MKK5DD expression; both tyrosine phosphorylation and the activation of MPK3 and MPK6 affected MT stability in salt stress response.
The kinase activity of MPK6 was totally turned off in mkk5, whereas the activity of MPK3 was only partially blocked.
salt tolerance is enhanced upon ectopic MPK3/MPK6 activation in an MKK5DD line.

133.q: The plasma membrane Na+/H+ exchange activity of transgenic yeast co-expressing CBL10, CIPK8, and SOS1 was higher than that of untransformed and SOS1 transgenic yeast, resulting in a lower Na+ accumulation and a better growth phenotype under salinity.

the CBL5-CIPK8 complex could positively regulate the activity of SOS1 in the reconstitution yeast system; Yeast cells coexpressing CBL5, CIPK8/CIPK24 and SOS1 demonstrated lesser Na+ accumulation and a better growth phenotype
Phosphorylation of SOS1 mediated by SOS3 (CBL4)-SOS2 (CIPK24) or CBL10-SOS2/CIPK8 protein kinase complexes is crucial for recovery of the Na+ extrusion activity of SOS1.

134.q: EIN2 positively regulates the functions of EIN3/EIL1 transcription factors, which results in the activation of transcription of ERF1 and other downstream genes.
CTR1p:GFP-ΔNT-gCTR1 seedlings showed constitutive expression of the ethylene-inducible ERF1 gene, to a level comparable to that observed in ctr1-2.
The expression level of ERF1 increased eightfold after 6 h of high salt treatment, which coincided with the EIN3 protein level.

135.q: Em1 and Em6—both encoding late embryogenesis abundant (LEA) proteins and directly targeted by ABI5 regulation—were expressed at very low levels in abi5-9 plants compared with the wild type.
facilitates the function of ABI5 to bind and activate the promoter of a target gene EM6

PBE1 modulates the protein level of the transcription factor ABI5 and thereby down-regulates the expression of several genes downstream of this key regulator… [including EM6/AT2G40170 listed as ABI5 downstream gene].

136.q: both SOD and CAT activities were somehow dramatically induced and maintained at high levels till the end of treatments... The enhanced ROS scavenging capability was consistent with that found in edt1 mutant and likely contributed to the lower level of malondialdehyde
stress-inducible expression of AtHDG11 resulted in up-regulation of Cu/Zn SOD and APX along with improved antioxidative enzyme activities in transgenic peanut lines under drought and salt stress.

137.q: H2 pretreatment modulated genes/proteins of zinc-finger transcription factor ZAT10/12 and related antioxidant defence enzymes, thus significantly counteracting the NaCl-induced reactive oxygen species (ROS) overproduction and lipid peroxidation.

Constitutive expression of the C2H2-EAR zinc finger protein Zat10 in Arabidopsis was found to elevate the expression of reactive oxygen-defense transcripts and to enhance the tolerance of plants to salinity, heat and osmotic stress.

139.q: AtWRKY30 overexpression in wheat significantly reduced the levels of oxidative stress biomarkers ... and enhanced the levels of ... enzymatic antioxidants, redox components ... indicating its general effect on stress tolerance and redox regulation.
Transgenic Arabidopsis plants constitutively overexpressing AtWRKY30 (35S::W30 lines) were more tolerant than wild-type plants to oxidative and salinity stresses during seed germination.

140.q: To monitor the ability of the CBL10 proteins to activate the SOS pathway, Saccharomyces cerevisiae strain AXT3K containing pYPGE15 with AtSOS1 and pFL32T with AtSOS2 and AtSOS3 was modified to express the CBL10 genes.

SCABP8 is further shown to bind calcium, interact with SOS2 both in vitro and in vivo, recruit SOS2 to the plasma membrane, enhance SOS2 activity in a calcium-dependent manner, and activate SOS1 in yeast.
the CALCINEURIN B-LIKE10 (AtCBL10) calcium sensor interacts with the AtSOS2 kinase to activate the AtSOS1 plasma membrane sodium/proton exchanger.
calcium sensor phosphorylation is a critical component of SOS pathway regulation of salt tolerance in Arabidopsis.

141.q: agb1 plants have higher root to shoot translocation rates of radiolabelled 24Na+ under transpiring conditions, as a result of larger stomatal apertures and increased stomatal conductance.
The agb1 mutant is hyposensitive to ABA inhibition of K+-mediated but not Na+-mediated stomatal opening.

142.q: At5ptase7 mutants demonstrated reduced production of reactive oxygen species (ROS). Supplementation of mutants with exogenous PtdIns dephosphorylated at the D5′ position restored ROS production.

143.q: sos1 mutants showed the opposite sodium distribution, with low sodium in the vacuoles and much higher sodium in the cytosol, producing a reversal of vacuole:cytosol ratios compared with WT. This revealed that the sos1 mutant is impaired in sodium translocation into vacuoles.

Under 50 mM NaCl treatment, all seven mutants exhibited reduced root length, lower fresh weight and compromised net Na+ efflux... This result validates an indispensable role of SOS1 in salt tolerance.
SOS3 interacts with and activates the protein kinase SOS2, which in turn activates the plasma membrane Na+/H+ antiporter SOS1 to bring about sodium ion homeostasis and salt tolerance

The Salt Overly Sensitive1 (SOS1) protein, which encodes a plasma membrane Na+/H+ antiporter... plays an important role in Na+ exclusion. Mutation of SOS1 led to a severely salt-sensitive phenotype due to impaired Na+ efflux.

The results suggested roles of the SOS1 protein, in addition to its function as a Na+/H+ antiporter, whose disruption affected membrane traffic and vacuolar functions possibly by controlling pH homeostasis in root cells.

tonoplast-localized SOS1 contributes to salt tolerance... we hypothesized that SOS1 may promote Na+ accumulation in vacuoles upon its targeting to the tonoplast, thereby reducing cytosolic Na+ concentrations.

145.q: PID OE lines showed severely reduced rosette growth and high auxin levels in the first pair of leaves. In the leaves of plants with ectopic PID expression an accumulation of auxin, oxidative burst and disruption of hormonal balance was apparent.
PA-regulated PINOID (PID) kinase is a crucial modulator of PIN2 activity and auxin redistribution in response to salt stress.

146.q: activation of JA signaling by salt stress occurs in the meristematic zone and stele of the differentiation zone and that this activation was dependent on JAR1 and proteasome functions.
the jar1 mutant with reduced bioactive JA-Ile accumulation showed enhanced salt stress tolerance

148.q: NaCl induces the unfolded protein response in the endoplasmic reticulum (ER) and cell cycle arrest in root tip cells of stt3a seedlings, as determined by expression profiling of ER stress–responsive chaperone (BiP-GUS) and cell division (CycB1;1-GUS) genes.
Unlike stt3a, cgl1 did not cause constitutive activation of the unfolded protein response.

151.q: NOE plants showed enhanced protection against cell damage due to the increased proline accumulation, preserved cell membrane integrity, enhanced reactive oxygen species (ROS) scavenging (e.g. increased superoxide dismutase activity), and reduced H2O2 and malondialdehyde (MDA) production.

152.q: bHLH35 regulated ethylene signaling pathways required for EL+HS acclimation (together with MYC2), as well as flavonoid metabolism and ethylene signaling pathways required for S+EL+HS (together with NAC069; Fig. 4f).
bHLH35 is required for the expression of transcripts associated with the regulation of flavonoid metabolism and ethylene signaling.

154.q: Transcriptome analysis shows upregulation of auxin synthesis, conjugation, transport and TRANSPORT INHIBITOR RESPONSE1 (TIR1) and AUXIN RESPONSE FACTOR (ARF) response genes, but downregulation of Aux/IAA response inhibitors.
highly increased auxin signaling by overexpression of mTIR1 may trigger auxin-mediated downstream pathways to enhance plant salt stress resistance.

157.q: Overexpression of the Arabidopsis SALT TOLERANT1 (STO1) gene leads to increased concentrations of 9-cis-epoxycarotenoid dioxygenase3, a vital enzyme in Arabidopsis abscisic acid biosynthesis.
significantly increasing the expression level of AtNCED3, promoting ABA biosynthesis and signal transduction, resulting in the significant up regulation of AtSNRK2.4 and AtPIF1/4 expression.
ABA accumulation is a key osmotic stress response, and it is dependent on the osmotic stress induction of the NCED3 gene, which encodes a rate-limiting enzyme in ABA biosynthesis.
These salt hypersensitive phenotypes can be rescued by... introducing an ABA-deficient mutant allele nced3 into iU1 transgenic plants.
Allantoin activates ABA production both through increased transcription of NCED3, encoding a key enzyme in ABA biosynthesis.
Overexpression of BG2 rescued the bg1 mutant phenotype, as observed for the overexpression of NCED3 in bg1 mutants.

158.q: ABA levels are increased both through de novo biosynthesis and via β-glucosidase homolog1 (BG1)-mediated hydrolysis of Glc-conjugated ABA (ABA-GE).

mannitol triggered increases in cytosolic ABA in the division zone and increases in ER ABA in the elongation and maturation zone within 1 h after treatment, both of which were abolished in the bg1-2 mutant, suggesting the requirement for BG1 in osmotic stress-triggered early ABA induction in Arabidopsis roots.

BG1, which encodes the enzyme BG1 that plays an important role in releasing free biologically active (+)-ABA from the inactive ABA glucose ester (ABA-GE), also decreased in Ler at 24 and 48 h after stress treatment in roots.

160.q: Salinity-induced ABI4 enhances RbohD expression by physically interacting with its promoter, and subsequently promotes ROS accumulation, thus resulting in cell membrane damage.

161.q: VPS23A is a critical regulator of the SOS pathway that positively modulates the regulation of salt tolerance.

162.q: The N-terminal domain of CC1 (CC1NTD) is sufficient to restore cellulose biosynthesis in Arabidopsis cc1cc2 knockout mutants.

163.q: the activity of reactive oxygen species (ROS)-scavenging enzymes was enhanced in S. miltiorrhiza via the overexpression of AtMYB12, and transgenic plants showed higher SOD, CAT, and POD activities.

164.q: the causal relationship of salt acclimation with haemin-induced HY1 expression and RbohD-derived ROS peak II formation... no ROS peak II or subsequent salt acclimatory responses were observed.

JA-responsive JAZ genes were up-regulated by salt stress in a COI1-dependent manner in the roots.

171.q: CYSTM3 could suppress the activities of ROS-scavenging enzymes and the transcription levels of salt responsive genes to dampen salt stress tolerance.
an analysis of CYSTM3 overexpression lines revealed negative regulation for this gene in salt stress responses

172.q: Knockout mutations of P5CS1 result in the reduction of stress-induced proline synthesis, hypersensitivity to salt stress, and accumulation of reactive oxygen species.
mutants having higher levels of lipid oxidation, H2O2 and superoxide (O2–) ... the accumulation was greater in p5cs1 mutants

FC1 and FC2 share 83% similarity and 69% identity at the amino acid level... belong to two distinct groups of plant ferrochelatases.

these two endosomal NHX antiporters share similar catalytic mode for ion transport.

222.q: The oxidative pentose phosphate pathway (OPPP) is the major pathway of the production of NADPH. The main regulatory step of OPPP is catalyzed by glucose-6-phosphate dehydrogenase (G6PDH or G6PD). The cy-G6PD includes G6PD5 and G6PD6.

235.q: the loss of function of STT3A causes a salt hypersensitive phenotype... indicating STT3A subunit isoform may mediate specific protein glycosylation during salinity stress

237.q: ATI1 in autophagic plastid-to-vacuole trafficking through its ability to interact with both plastid proteins and ATG8 of the core autophagy machinery.

238.q: Galactinol synthase (GolS; EC: 2.4.1.123) and Raffinose Synthase, which are responsible for raffinose biosynthesis, play a key role in response to abiotic stresses.

245.q: we identified the small GTPase LIGHT INSENSITIVE PERIOD 1 (LIP1) as a circadian-clock-associated protein that regulates light input to the clock
The small GTPase LIGHT INSENSITIVE PERIOD1 (LIP1) is a circadian clock-associated protein that regulates light input to the clock.

250.q: Ethylene is a gaseous plant hormone perceived by a family of receptors in Arabidopsis including ETHYLENE RESPONSE1 (ETR1) and ETR2.
loss of ETHYLENE RESPONSE1 (ETR1) or ETHYLENE INSENSITIVE4 (EIN4) leads to accelerated germination, loss of ETR2 delays germination

252.q: HsfA2, which is induced by heat, high light, osmotic and oxidative stress, has been proposed to respond to multiple stress signaling pathways.
These results indicate that the HsfA2 gene plays a role in the HS stress response.

Analysis of putative target genes of AtHSFA2 through quantitative RT-PCR showed higher and constitutive expression of several abiotic stress responsive genes in transgenic Arabidopsis plants over-expressing TaHsfA2d.

253.q: GB is synthesized through a two-step oxidation of choline via betaine aldehyde. Two enzymes, CMO and NAD-dependent betaine aldehyde dehydrogenase (BADH), are involved.
ALDH10A8 and ALDH10A9 that code for betaine aldehyde dehydrogenases (BADHs). BADHs were initially known to catalyze the last step of the biosynthesis of GB in plants.

254.q: GB is synthesized through a two-step oxidation of choline via betaine aldehyde. Two enzymes, CMO and NAD-dependent betaine aldehyde dehydrogenase (BADH), are involved.
ALDH10A8 and ALDH10A9 that code for betaine aldehyde dehydrogenases (BADHs). BADHs were initially known to catalyze the last step of the biosynthesis of GB in plants.

255.q: These data together with the reduction in cell elongation and proliferation observed in the ttl1 mutant under osmotic stress support a role for TTL1 in maintaining cell wall physical properties necessary for sustaining a proper root growth during osmotic stress.
High NaCl concentrations led to less crystalline cellulose content in ttl1ttl3 as compared to WT roots. Collectively, these results link TTL1 and TTL3 to cellulose synthesis under salt stress.

257.q: A tno1 mutant has defects in vacuolar trafﬁcking... tno1 mutant partially mislocalizes vacuolar proteins to the apoplast, indicating a role in vacuolar trafﬁcking.
Vacuolar proteins were partially secreted to the apoplast in the tno1 mutant, suggesting that TNO1 is required for efficient protein trafficking to the vacuole.

258.q: alterations in the protein levels of the AsA biosynthetic enzyme GDP-Man pyrophosphorylase (VTC1) are associated with changes in AsA contents in light and darkness.
GDP-Man pyrophosphorylase—known as GMP in tomato and VTC1 in Arabidopsis—is a key enzyme in this pathway.

260.q: These results suggest that CIPK8-mediated activation of SOS1 is CBL10-dependent and SOS3-independent, indicating that CIPK8 and SOS2 activity in shoots is sufficient for regulating Arabidopsis salt tolerance.
CBL5 functions in seed germination and protects seeds and germinating seedlings from salt stress through the CBL5‐CIPK8/CIPK24‐SOS1 pathways.

262.q: the canonical auxin perception comprises six receptors that lead to the activation of target genes: TRANSPORT INHIBITOR RESPONSE 1 (TIR1) and five AUXIN F-BOX (AFB) proteins, AFB1 to AFB5
AFB1, AFB2 and AFB3 are the F-box subunits of SCF E3-ubiquitin ligase complex and function as auxin receptors

263.q: Compared to the wild type, roots of the triple abcg2-1 abcg6-1 abcg20-1 mutant roots have a distorted suberin lamellae structure and major reductions in alkyl hydroxycinnamates of suberin-associated waxes.
suberin monomers are synthesized in the ER, they are transported outside of the plasma membrane through ATP binding cassette (ABC) transporter proteins (ABCG2, ABCG6, and ABCG20; Yadav et al., 2014).

264.q: suberin monomers are synthesized in the ER, they are transported outside of the plasma membrane through ATP binding cassette (ABC) transporter proteins (ABCG2, ABCG6, and ABCG20; Yadav et al., 2014).
Mutants selected in this study were affected in suberin monomer transport (abcg2-1 abcg6-1 abcg20-1)

265.q: suberin monomers are synthesized in the ER, they are transported outside of the plasma membrane through ATP binding cassette (ABC) transporter proteins (ABCG2, ABCG6, and ABCG20; Yadav et al., 2014).
Mutants selected in this study were affected in suberin monomer transport (abcg2-1 abcg6-1 abcg20-1)

266.q: The IP-MS data revealed several cellulose biosynthesis–related components, such as CESA1, CESA2, CESA3, CESA5, CESA6, CESA9, and CSI1, highlighting the prominent role of CC1 in regulating cellulose biosynthesis.
Since CC1 can directly interact with the CesA proteins in yeast, and the CCs track together with the CSC in the plasma membrane, we propose that the CCs constitute members of the CSC.

274.q: the three downstream enzymes N-acetylserotonin methyltransferase (ASMT), caffeic acid O-methyltransferase (COMT) and serotonin N-acetyltransferase (SNAT)... SNAT and then ASMT converted N-acetylserotonin to MEL

a double mutant of Arabidopsis (snat1snat2) defective in melatonin biosynthesis genes SNAT1 and SNAT2... The content of melatonin... in snat1snat2 were drastically reduced compared with the wild type.
SNAT acts as the rate-limiting enzyme in the overall melatonin biosynthetic pathway in Arabidopsis
In plants, melatonin biosynthesis is catalyzed from serotonin by two key enzymes, serotonin N-acetyltransferase (SNAT) and N-acetylserotonin methyltransferase (ASMT).

275.q: ASMT and SNAT were S-sulfhydrated by NaHS (H2S donor), but not COMT1. The S-sulfhydration levels of ASMT and SNAT increased along with the NaHS concentration.
SNAT catalyzes serotonin into N-acetylserotonin in chloroplasts, whereas ASMT could participate in synthesizing melatonin from N-acetylserotonin in the cytoplasm.

276.q: cytokinin perception is mediated by cytokinin receptor Arabidopsis histidine kinases [AHK2, AHK3 and AHK4/CRE1]
the three cytokinin (CK) receptor histidine kinases AHK2, AHK3 and AHK4/CRE1

277.q: cytokinin perception is mediated by cytokinin receptor Arabidopsis histidine kinases [AHK2, AHK3 and AHK4/CRE1]
the three cytokinin (CK) receptor histidine kinases AHK2, AHK3 and AHK4/CRE1

278.q: cytokinin perception is mediated by cytokinin receptor Arabidopsis histidine kinases [AHK2, AHK3 and AHK4/CRE1]
the three cytokinin (CK) receptor histidine kinases AHK2, AHK3 and AHK4/CRE1
cytokinin clearly mediates stress responses because it was required for CRE1 to function as a negative regulator of osmotic stress.

279.q: PDF1.2 and VSP1 that are well know markers for ... JA/ET-mediated defense pathways
B. japonicum also influenced reductions of RD22, MSD1, DHAR and MYC2 in shoots and DHAR, ADC2, RD20, RD29B, GTR1, ANAC055, VSP1 and VSP2 gene expression in roots under salt stress.

280.q: VSP2-At-F ... Response gene to JA in Arabidopsis
B. japonicum also influenced reductions of RD22, MSD1, DHAR and MYC2 in shoots and DHAR, ADC2, RD20, RD29B, GTR1, ANAC055, VSP1 and VSP2 gene expression in roots under salt stress.

281.q: MIK2 has both overlapping and distinct functions with THE1, a malectin-like receptor kinase previously proposed as cell wall integrity sensor.
THE1 has been described to be responsible for sensing CW alterations and mechanical stress responses upon cellulose impairment.

282.q: The NaCl tolerance conferred by AtNHX proteins correlated with greater Na+ content, indicative of ion compartmentalization rather than extrusion. AtNHX2 has a major function in vacuolar compartmentalization of Na+.
Kinetic analysis of K+ and Na+ transport indicated that AtNHX1 and AtNHX2 are the main contributors to both vacuolar pH and K+ and Na+ uptake, while AtNHX3 and AtNHX4 differ in Na+/K+ selectivity.
Intracellular NHX proteins are Na+,K+/H+ antiporters involved in K+ homeostasis, endosomal pH regulation, and salt tolerance.
the Arabidopsis vacuolar (Na+,K+)/H+ antiporters AtNHX1 and AtNHX2 were considered for a long time to have a main role in salt tolerance through Na+ transport from the cytosol to the vacuole

283.q: arf7/19 double mutant lack the growth response to auxin and show a significantly reduced sensitivity to osmotic stress.
arf7arf19 and tir1afb2afb3, defective in transcription factors or receptors involved in auxin response ... the arf7arf19 and tir1afb2afb3 mutants were clearly oversensitive to this salt treatment.

286.q: inhibiting the expressions of auxin signaling response factor GH3.2/3.10/3.12
Group II GH3 enzymes (GH3.1, GH3.2, GH3.3, GH3.4, GH3.5, GH3.6, GH3.9 and GH3.17) are known to modulate IAA concentrations redundantly by catalysing the formation of IAA-aa conjugates.

289.q: We also confirmed that two differentially abundant peroxidases, PRX32 and PRX34, were N-glycosylation substrates and function redundantly in salt tolerance by root growth assay and hydrogen peroxide (H2O2) measurement.

290.q: the zinc-finger transcription factor ZAT10/12 plays a key role in abiotic stress tolerance, which could be attributed to the specific activation of antioxidant defence genes
Downregulation of ZAT10/12-mediated antioxidant gene expression (cAPX1/2 and FSD1) was observed after 7 d of treatment.

291.q: The calcium sensor CBL10 mediates salt tolerance by regulating ion homeostasis in Arabidopsis
The tonoplast association of CBL10 depends on Arabidopsis PAT10 and protein S‐acylation... CBL10/SCaBP8 is critical for salt tolerance and associates with the tonoplast.
this phosphorylation...enhanced plasma membrane Na+/H+ exchange activity.

292.q: RDR2 is involved in the biogenesis of 24 nt heterochromatic siRNAs and is the only RDR protein that functions in the RNA-directed DNA methylation (RdDM) pathway that facilitates heterochromatin formation through DNA methylation and histone modifications.
non-CG methylation, in particular asymmetric CHH methylation in the AtHKT1 promoter, is mediated by small RNAs generated through the RDR2 pathway.

293.q: LOX1-At-F ... Synthesis gene of JA in Arabidopsis
This demonstrated that 9-LOX was involved in the JA synthesis under osmotic stress.

294.q: Group II GH3 enzymes (GH3.1, GH3.2, GH3.3, GH3.4, GH3.5, GH3.6, GH3.9 and GH3.17) are known to modulate IAA concentrations redundantly by catalysing the formation of IAA-aa conjugates, which are inactive IAA forms.
GH3.3 has been reported to encode an IAA-amido synthase that quickly responds to auxin and is positively correlated with adventitious root development.

296.q: Biosynthesis begins with tryptophan and a series of enzymes are involved... N-acetylserotonin methyltransferase (ASMT), caffeic acid O-methyltransferase (COMT) and serotonin N-acetyltransferase (SNAT).
the expression of SNAT1, COMT1, and ASMT1, three key genes involved in melatonin biosynthesis, was determined using qPCR

299.q: EIN3/EIL1 are subject to ubiquitin/proteasome-mediated proteolysis that requires two F-box proteins, EBF1/EBF2; high salinity induced the accumulation of EIN3/EIL1 by promoting the proteasomal degradation of EBF1 and EBF2.
In the nucleus, EBF1 and EBF2 degrade EIN3 via the 26S proteasome.

300.q: Na+/H+-exchange activity was greatly reduced in vesicles of the salt-sensitive sos1-1 mutant, and was significantly higher in the salt-tolerant gi-1 mutant. GI appears to act as a negative regulator of salt tolerance by inhibiting Na+/H+ exchanger activity of SOS1.
GI protein directly binds to the SOS2 kinase which is then prevented from phosphorylating and thus activating the antiporter activity of SOS1.

301.q: MPK3/6 activities are significantly higher in mkp1 than in Col-0 WT. We assessed the activation status of MPK3/6 under osmo-shock stress and found that it was higher in aod13 than in Bu-5 WT.
the activity level of one of these, MPK6, is regulated by MKP1 in vivo... MKPs... their negative regulators, MAP kinase phosphatases.

302.q: The activation of KAI2 triggers its association with MAX2 and SMAX1/SMXL2, leading to SMAX1 and SMXL2 degradation.

This enzyme complex ubiquitinates downstream signaling components SUPPRESSOR OF MAX2 1 (SMAX1) and SMAX1-LIKE 2 (SMXL2), resulting in their proteasomal degradation for initiating KAI2 signaling responses.

303.q: the transcript level of AtNHX1 increased by 43% and 106% in atsos1 and athkt1;1, respectively, compared with WT

We interpret this to mean that expression of AtHKT1;1 in the hkt1-1 null mutant is reduced to such an extent that leaf Na+ accumulation saturates the capacity for cellular detoxification of Na+ by vacuolar compartmentalization... an active leaf vacuolar Na+ compartmentalization mechanism driven by AtNHX1 is one likely candidate.

304.q: The expression of FT, and of genes that act later in the flowering pathway, such as AP1, CAL, FUL, and LFY, was greatly repressed in the transgenic plants.
Overexpression of an active NTL8 form exhibited delayed flowering... expression of FLOWERING LOCUS T (FT) and its downstream genes was significantly reduced in the transgenic plants.

305.q: two transcription factors BPC1/BPC2 were found to directly bind the GALS1 promoter, to repress GALS1 expression, resulting in decreased b-1,4-galactan accumulation and increased salt tolerance.
NaCl relieves the direct suppression of GALS1 transcription by the transcription factors BPC1/BPC2 and thereby induces the excess accumulation of galactan.

306.q: two transcription factors BPC1/BPC2 were found to directly bind the GALS1 promoter, to repress GALS1 expression, resulting in decreased b-1,4-galactan accumulation and increased salt tolerance.
both CBF1/CBF2/CBF3 and BPC1/BPC2 directly repress GALS1 transcription to regulate the salt response.

307.q: this interaction promotes ubiquitination-dependent VTC1 degradation through the 26S proteasome pathway. Consistent with this, csn5b mutants showed very high AsA levels in both light and darkness.
CSN5B... can affect AsA biosynthesis and subsequently modulate plant responses to abiotic stress by binding to VTC1 and promoting its degradation in the dark.

308.q: In hda19 plants, the expression levels of stress tolerance-related genes, late embryogenesis abundant proteins and positive regulators such as ABI5 and NAC019 in abscisic acid signaling, were induced strongly relative to the wild type.
In hda19-3 plants, positive regulators, such as NAC019 and ABI5 in the ABA signaling pathway, that contribute to enhanced environmental stress tolerance are strongly upregulated.

309.q: In hda19 plants, the expression levels of stress tolerance-related genes... positive regulators such as ABI5 and NAC019 in abscisic acid signaling, were induced strongly relative to the wild type.
positive regulators, such as NAC019 and ABI5 in the ABA signaling pathway, that contribute to enhanced environmental stress tolerance are strongly upregulated in hda19-3 plants.

310.q: Expression was increased in prt6, at a similar level to prt6 ucp1, which indicates that PRT6 acts downstream of UCP1 in controlling hypoxia gene expression.
Consistent with the ERF-VII gene expression data presented here, ADH1 transcript was highly accumulated in the prt6 mutant under non-stress and salinity conditions.

311.q: APETALA 1 (AP1) gene, which is a downstream target of the FT–FD module, is suppressed in BFT-overexpressing plants (Yoo et al., 2010).
expression of the APETALA1 (AP1) gene was suppressed to a lesser degree in the bft-2 mutant than in Col-0 plants

312.q: COP1 exerts its function by destabilizing various transcriptional regulators including Long After Far-red light 1 (LAF1) and Elongated Hypocotyl 5 (HY5), which are involved in photomorphogenesis.
salt stress and ethylene antagonistically regulate nucleocytoplasmic partitioning of COP1, thereby controlling Arabidopsis seed germination via the COP1-mediated down-regulation of HY5 and ABI5.

313.q: Their transcript levels were decreased in lines overexpressing miR408 and increased in lines with suppressed miR408 expression. This analysis further confirmed that these four genes are in vivo targets of miR408.
RT-qPCR quantification of LAC3 transcript abundance, respective target gene of miR408, in 15-day-old Col-0, drb1, and drb2 seedlings following 7-day salt stress treatment period.

314.q: The VTC2 expression was obviously inhibited at the beginning of 4 h of salt stress in Col-0, while the inhibition was impaired in abi4–103 mutants, suggesting that the inhibited VTC2 transcription is partially ABI4 dependent.
ABI4 directly binds to the promoter of VTC2 and represses its transcription (Yu et al., 2019). Fig. S12 ABI4 negatively regulates VTC2 transcription.

315.q: SOS2 phosphorylates FREE1, leading to its degradation and affecting MVB maturation, thereby reducing MVB-vacuole fusion and regulating endomembrane dynamics in response to salt stress.
FREE1 is phosphorylated by SOS2, leading to its degradation and subsequent vacuole fragmentation under salt stress.

316.q: VIR-mediated m6A methylation modulates reactive oxygen species homeostasis by negatively regulating the mRNA stability of several salt stress negative regulators, including ATAF1, GI and GSTU17, through affecting 3ʹ-UTR lengthening linked to alternative polyadenylation.
GSTU17, IPGAM2, AOX1a, and AKR4C9 were methylated by both FIO1 and VIR.

317.q: ABA does not reduce the level of AFBm:GUS expression... miR393-resistant TIR1m and AFB2m seedlings were insensitive to the inhibition of lateral root development caused by ABA and PEG treatment.
NaCl-mediated salt stress induces miR393 expression by enhancing the transcription of AtMIR393A and leads to a concomitant reduction in the levels of the TIR1 and AFB2 receptors.
the targets of miR393 are F-box genes that encode auxin receptors [TIR1, AFB2 and AFB3]... we generated a miR393-resistant TIR1 gene (mTIR1)
the known auxin receptor clade transcripts TIR1/AFB2 are the target for the miR393s.

318.q: stress-regulated miR393-guided cleavage of the transcripts encoding two auxin receptors, TIR1 and AFB2, was required for inhibition of lateral root growth by ABA or osmotic stress.
NaCl-mediated salt stress induces miR393 expression by enhancing the transcription of AtMIR393A and leads to a concomitant reduction in the levels of the TIR1 and AFB2 receptors.
the known auxin receptor clade transcripts TIR1/AFB2 are the target for the miR393s.

319.q: Several potential SnRK2.10 targets phosphorylated in response to stress were identified by a phosphoproteomic approach, including the dehydrins ERD10 and ERD14. Their phosphorylation by SnRK2.10 was confirmed in vitro.
Two dehydrins, Early Responsive to Dehydration 10 and 14 (ERD10 and ERD14), were identified as direct substrates of SnRK2.10 (Maszkowska et al., 2019).

320.q: Several potential SnRK2.10 targets phosphorylated in response to stress were identified by a phosphoproteomic approach, including the dehydrins ERD10 and ERD14. Their phosphorylation by SnRK2.10 was confirmed in vitro.
Two dehydrins, Early Responsive to Dehydration 10 and 14 (ERD10 and ERD14), were identified as direct substrates of SnRK2.10 (Maszkowska et al., 2019).

321.q: the ER-localized ethylene receptors activate CTR1 protein kinase, which in turn phosphorylates ETHYLENE INSENSITIVE 2 (EIN2)...CTR1-mediated phosphorylation of EIN2 prevents EIN2 from signaling in the absence of ethylene.
in vitro purified CTR1-MBP can phosphorylate EIN2 CEND-MBP

322.q: SOS2 phosphorylates the ESCRT-I component FREE1, facilitating its degradation as reported in our previous study (Liu et al., 2025). This regulatory pathway enhances the sorting of SOS1 to the tonoplast.
SOS2 phosphorylates FREE1, leading to its degradation and affecting MVB maturation, thereby reducing MVB-vacuole fusion and regulating endomembrane dynamics in response to salt stress.

323.q: Both MBP and MYB44 were phosphorylated by MPK3. These findings indicate that MYB44 and MPK3 interact in vitro, and that one or several residues in MYB44 are targeted by the kinase.
MPK3 phosphorylates MYB44, thereby stimulating MYB44 function as transcriptional inducer.

324.q: HSFA4A can be phosphorylated not only by mitogen-activated protein (MAP) kinases MPK3 and MPK6 but also by MPK4, and Ser309 is the dominant MAP kinase phosphorylation site.
MPK3 and MPK6 phosphorylate HSFA4A in vitro on three distinct sites, serine-309 being the major phosphorylation site.

325.q: HSFA4A can be phosphorylated not only by mitogen-activated protein (MAP) kinases MPK3 and MPK6 but also by MPK4, and Ser309 is the dominant MAP kinase phosphorylation site.
MPK3 and MPK6 phosphorylate HSFA4A in vitro on three distinct sites, serine-309 being the major phosphorylation site.

326.q: When exposed to osmotic stress, SA accumulation and the defence marker genes PR1 and EDS1 (SA dependent) and PR2 (SA independent) were strongly induced in Col-0 but not in NIL-Bu-5 plants.
ACQOS contributes to resistance to bacteria, but causes detrimental autoimmunity under osmotic stress via the TIR-NLR immunity regulators EDS1 and PAD4, thereby reducing osmotolerance.

327.q: Karrikin induced expression of DLK2 and KUF1 genes and inhibited hypocotyl elongation independently of osmotic stress.
The expression of AtDLK2 is strongly down-regulated in kai2 and max2 mutant plants, and is subjected to the regulation by SMAX1 and SMXL2, rather than SMXL6,7 and 8.

328.q: H2 pretreatment modulated genes/proteins of zinc-finger transcription factor ZAT10/12 and related antioxidant defence enzymes... transcript levels of ZAT10, ZAT12, cAPX1 and FSD1 after 120 hr of indicated treatments were analyzed by real-time RT-PCR.
Downregulation of ZAT10/12-mediated antioxidant gene expression (cAPX1/2 and FSD1) was observed after 7 d of treatment.

329.q: zinc-finger transcription factor ZAT10/12 plays a key role in abiotic stress tolerance, which could be attributed to the specific activation of antioxidant defence genes, e.g. cAPX1 and Fe superoxide dismutase (FSD1).
Downregulation of ZAT10/12-mediated antioxidant gene expression (cAPX1/2 and FSD1) was observed after 7 d of treatment.

330.q: loss-of-function of AtSOS1 induced a 56–62% (6–24 h) decrease in transcripts of AtHKT1;1 ... in the roots
Na+ recirculation—where Na+ is transported from leaves back to the root—is mainly mediated by HKT1s; SOS1 excludes ~90–95% of phloem-derived Na+ from mature root zone.
HKT1 expression decreased in both Col3 and sos1-1 under salt stress, with a much stronger decrease in sos1-1 to nearly undetectable mRNA levels by 8 h of salt stress.

331.q: Salt-induced proline accumulation and P5CS1 expression were reduced in the hy5hyh double mutant, suggesting that HY5 promotes proline biosynthesis through connecting light and stress signals.
Deletion of the C/A-box or hy5 hyh mutations caused rapid reduction of H3K4me3 level at P5CS1 during the recovery stage, resulting in impairment of the stress memory response.

332.q: Activated SOS2 protein added in vitro increased tonoplast Na+/H+-exchange activity in vesicles isolated from sos2 but did not have any effect on activity in vesicles isolated from wild type, sos1, or sos3.
SOS2 can also phosphorylate AtNHX1 on vacuolar membranes and activate AtNHX1. Therefore, SOS2 is a regulator for AtNHX1, linking the two salt signaling pathways.

333.q: The kinase activity of MPK6 was totally turned off in mkk5, whereas the activity of MPK3 was only partially blocked.
salt tolerance is enhanced upon ectopic MPK3/MPK6 activation in an MKK5DD line.

334.q: Indeed, strains expressing both CAX1 and SOS2 grew in a manner similar to sCAX1; A purified GST-SOS2 fusion protein added directly to vacuolar membrane preparations from CAX1-expressing yeast cells caused a dose-dependent activation of H+/Ca2+ antiport.
Signaling molecules that regulate H+/Na+ transport may also regulate CAX transporters (Cheng et al. 2004).

335.q: In the mkk2 null line, cold-induced activation of MPK4 was almost undetectable, whereas MPK6 activation was significantly reduced, but not completely abrogated.
Overexpression of MKK2 leads to constitutive MPK4 and MPK6 activities and the increase of salt and freezing tolerance. Yeast two-hybrid assays reveal that MPK4 and MPK6 are MKK2 targets.

336.q: The cgl1 mutation strongly enhanced the phenotype of a temperature sensitive allele of KOR1/RSW2 (rsw2-1)... This establishes that plant complex N-glycan modification is important for the in vivo function of KOR1/RSW2.
aberrant modification of the plasma membrane glycoprotein KORRIGAN 1/RADIALLY SWOLLEN 2 (KOR1/RSW2) that is necessary for cellulose biosynthesis occurred in cgl1 and stt3a.

337.q: Localization of SYP61, which is involved in the salt stress response, was disrupted in the tno1 mutant.
The SNARE SYP61, which forms a t-SNARE complex with SYP41, is mis-localized in the tno1 mutant.

338.q: UBP16 stabilizes SHM1 by removing the conjugated ubiquitin. Ser hydroxymethyltransferase activity is lower in the ubp16 mutant than in the wild type but higher than in the shm1 mutant.
UBP16 ... at least partially modulating SERINE HYDROXYMETHYLTRANSFERASE1 (SHM1) stability and activity

339.q: significant reduced induction was observed in both UCP1ox rap2.2 rap2.3 versus UCP1ox ... these data indicate that UCP1 acts through ERFVIIs in regulating hypoxia gene expression
ADH1 is a downstream gene of most ERF-VIIs; overexpression of HRE1, RAP2.2, RAP2.3, or RAP2.12 upregulates the expression of ADH1 even under non-stress conditions.

340.q: In atrbohD mutants, haemin pre-treatment resulted in induction of HY1 expression, but no similar response was seen in hy1-100, and no ROS peak II... HY1 plays an important role in salt acclimation signalling, and requires participation of RbohD-derived ROS peak II.
Arabidopsis HO1 (HY1 or AtHO1) was also found to be involved in salt acclimation, which required the participation of RbohD-derived ROS [5].

341.q: after 3 h of treatment with 300 mM Mannitol, the three genes are up-regulated in the wild-type plants. In mutant plants, the inductions of HB7 and PP2C were not completely abolished and were near 50% of those observed in wild-type plants.
the mRNA levels of LTP3, RD20, HB7, and NAM-like genes... transcript levels of these genes decreased in bzip17 mutants in comparison to wild type plants exposed to salt.

342.q: GA2ox7 (At1g50960) was dramatically increased (223-fold) in the transgenic plants... upregulation of GA2ox7 by DDF1 causes GA deﬁciency in transgenic DDF1-overexpressing plants.
The DDF1 transcriptional activator upregulates expression of a gibberellin-deactivating gene, GA2ox7, under high-salinity stress in Arabidopsis.

343.q: Several salt stress genes, known to be expressed downstream of AtbZIP17 from our previous study, were up-regulated under unstressed conditions in 35S:bZIP17DC seedlings. The genes included the transcription factors, ATHB-7 and a NAM-like transcription factor.
AtbZIP17 activation directly or indirectly upregulated the expression of several salt stress response genes, including the homeodomain transcription factor ATHB-7.
The transcript levels of these genes decreased in bzip17 mutants in comparison to wild type plants exposed to salt, confirming the findings of Liu et al. [2007a].
In mutant plants, the inductions of HB7 and PP2C were not completely abolished and were near 50% of those observed in wild-type plants.

344.q: Further analyses with biochemical and molecular approaches revealed that EIN3 physically binds to the ESE1 promoter, demonstrating that ESE1 was one target of EIN3.
EIN3 S325A mutation reduced its transcriptional activating activity on ESE1 promoter:GUS in a transient GUS assay

345.q: ChIP (Chromatin immunoprecipitation) assays confirmed that LHY can bind the promoter of ABF3 in vivo. ... the expression of ABF1 and ABF3 are indeed altered in LHY-OX and cca1-1 lhy-20
In vitro confirmation of LHY binding to the ABI1, ABF3 and SNRK2.2 promoters.

346.q: Chromatin immunoprecipitation and electrophoretic mobility shift assays demonstrated that the transcription factor ELONGATED HYPOCOTYL 5 (HY5) binds to G-box and C-box elements of P5CS1 and a C-box motif of PDH1.
HY5 binds directly to light-responsive element, C/A-box, in the P5CS1 promoter. Deletion of the C/A-box or hy5 hyh mutations caused rapid reduction of H3K4me3 level at P5CS1 during the recovery stage

347.q: auxin is linked via the IAA30 gene with a salt signaling cascade mediated by the NAM-ATAF1/2-CUC2 transcription factor NTM2… IAA30 gene, which was induced by salt in a NTM2-dependent manner.
NTM2 mediates the signaling crosstalk between auxin and salt stress via the IAA30 gene during seed germination; IAA30 was induced by high salinity in a NTM2-dependent manner.

348.q: over-expression of DRB3 suppressed the expression of PAP1, CHS, DFR and ANS genes... key transcription factor PAP1 for anthocyanin biosynthesis.
The transcript levels of CHS and DFR...were higher in pap1-D plants than in Col-0 plants (Borevitz et al. 2000).

349.q: Under the three-stress combination, bHLH35 interacts with NAC069, binds the promoter of lateral organ boundaries domain 31 (LBD31), and regulates the expression of transcripts involved in flavonoid metabolism and ethylene signaling.
in the Y1H assay bHLH35 showed binding to the LBD31 promoter (Fig. 4a), in the EMSA study it needed to interact with NAC069 before it could bind the LBD31 promoter (Fig. 4b).

350.q: Activation of the MPK3 and MPK6 mitogen-activated protein kinase pathway led to the transcriptional activation of the HEAT SHOCK PROTEIN17.6A gene... mutation of serine-309 to alanine strongly diminished phosphorylation of HSFA4A, it also strongly reduced the transcriptional activation of HEAT SHOCK PROTEIN17.6A.
Chromatin immunoprecipitation assays confirmed binding of HSFA4A to promoters of target genes encoding the small heat shock protein HSP17.6A and transcription factors WRKY30 and ZAT12.

351.q: HY5 directly binds to the promoter of ABSCISIC ACID INSENSITIVE 5 (ABI5) and upregulates transcription, thereby positively regulating ABA signalling.
salt treatment stimulated and ethylene suppressed transcription of ABA INSENSITIVE5 (ABI5), which was directly transcriptionally regulated by HY5.

352.q: a directed edge from ERF6 to STZ means that STZ was significantly differentially expressed... rendered a network of which the edges are based on the differentially expressed genes in every GOF line
ERF6 also activates the expression of a plethora of osmotic stress-responsive genes, including the well-known stress tolerance genes STZ, MYB51, and WRKY33.

353.q: In leaves of Arabidopsis plants overexpressing heat shock transcription factor A2 (HsfA2), the transcription of GolS1, -2, and -4 and raffinose synthase 2 (RS2) was highly induced
